key_findings: All required metric data is available in scan_persistence.db after WO-00022 and WO-00023 migrations — zero new schema changes required for WO-00024
run_full_dns_pass.sh emits CHUNK_START/CHUNK_DONE log events (lines 17, 28) but these are text-only; chunk_queue.completed_at (WO-00022) is the canonical queryable progress store
ingest_runs table (persistence_gateway.py lines 91-101) already tracks per-run error counts — feeds ALT-02 persistence error alert without additional instrumentation
Hourly report fields from PHASE_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md lines 152-160 map 1:1 to chunk_queue + event_sync_ledger + sink_outbox queries — no ambiguity in source attribution
ETA is computed over rolling 1-hour completion rate window (not cumulative average) for responsiveness to actual pipeline speed changes
ALT-01 (NoChunkProgress) uses 5-minute eval sweep despite 30-minute threshold — tight polling catches the stall early and avoids alerting exactly at the 30-minute boundary
ALT-03 (DeadLetterAccumulation) fires immediately on dlq_unreviewed >= 1 — DLQ entries are discrete incidents, not rate thresholds, and require immediate human attention
MEDIUM alerts require 2 consecutive positive evaluations before firing — reduces false alert rate below 5% target for transient metric spikes

decisions.decision: SQLite as sole metric source (not log files)
decisions.rationale: Log files are append-only text requiring parsing; SQLite provides structured, timestamped, transactionally-consistent data queryable without parsing logic; chunk_queue.completed_at is authoritative

decisions.decision: Hourly report is a cumulative snapshot (monotonic counters)
decisions.rationale: Monotonically non-decreasing counters are simpler to validate — they can only increase; downstream consumers compute deltas; prevents negative rate bugs from double-counting
Most recent 1-hour rate is more responsive than cumulative average; reflects actual pipeline speed at time of reporting; NULL when rate_per_h = 0 rather than infinity

decisions.decision: Alert evaluator runs every 5 minutes (not hourly)
decisions.rationale: ALT-01 threshold is 30 minutes — hourly evaluation would have up to 60-minute detection latency; 5-minute sweep guarantees ≤35 minute detection latency for no-progress condition
Single transient metric spike should not wake an operator; two consecutive cycles above threshold (10 minutes apart) confirms a real trend; exception for DLQ (immediate always)
When all 51 chunks complete, ALT-01 and ALT-06 would permanently fire (no further progress expected); suppression rules prevent false alerts after successful pipeline completion

monitoring_queries.composite_description: Single-pass hourly status matrix — runs all counters in one SQLite session for consistency

alert_rules.name: DeadLetterAccumulation
alert_rules.source: WO-00023 DLQ design: any unreviewed DLQ entry requires human review

alert_rules.name: SinkBacklogAge
alert_rules.source: WO-00023 success metric: sink backlog oldest age <2h (steady state)

alert_rules.name: CoverageStalled
alert_rules.threshold: coverage_pct unchanged for 2 consecutive hourly reports
alert_rules.source: WO-00024 success metric: hourly report continuity 100%

alert_rules.name: ReportMissing
alert_rules.threshold: hourly report file not present after 5 min past hour boundary
alert_rules.source: WO-00024 success metric: hourly report continuity 100%

implementation_sequence.action: Deploy WO-00022 schema_patch.sql + WO-00023 sqlite_schema_migration.sql if not already applied
Create reports/ directory and generate_hourly_report.sh that runs MON-11 and writes hourly_<YYYYMMDDTHH>.json
Implement alert_evaluator.py consuming alert_policy.yaml — runs every 5 minutes, evaluates all ALT-01..07 conditions
Add cron: `*/5 * * * * python3 alert_evaluator.py` for continuous alert sweep
Run continuity drill: verify 2+ consecutive reports emitted with monotonically increasing coverage_pct
Run no-progress drill: pause chunk processing, verify ALT-01 fires within 35 minutes
Run DLQ drill: manually insert dead_letter_events row, verify ALT-03 fires on next eval cycle

cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00022: chunk_queue table and completed_at timestamps required for ALT-01, MON-01, MON-07, MON-08, MON-09
cross_wo_dependencies.WO-00023: sink_outbox, event_sync_ledger, dead_letter_events required for ALT-03, ALT-04, ALT-05, MON-03, MON-04, MON-05, MON-10

risk_model.risk: Alert evaluator process crashes silently — alerts not evaluated
risk_model.mitigation: Run evaluator under supervisord or systemd with auto-restart; ALT-07 (ReportMissing) catches report generator failures; add evaluator heartbeat to logs

risk_model.risk: CoverageStalled (ALT-06) fires as false alert when pipeline completes
risk_model.mitigation: Suppression rule: suppress ALT-06 when chunks.completed == 51; evaluator checks suppression rules before firing

risk_model.risk: ETA computation returns NULL when no chunks complete in last 1h
risk_model.mitigation: By design: NULL eta_h is correct when rate_per_h=0; report consumers display 'ETA: unknown (pipeline stalled)' and ALT-01 will fire concurrently

risk_model.risk: MON-11 composite query takes too long on 10M+ event table
risk_model.mitigation: All metrics use COUNT(*) on indexed columns (event_sync_ledger.sink_target indexed, sink_outbox.status indexed); SQLite COUNT is O(table_scan) but fast on warm cache; measure query time and add covering index if >2s
